summary: Refined several Fighter struct members from integer placeholders to pointer types so newly matched Fighter_Unload_8006DABC could call HSD cleanup/free routines without explicit casts. The change also initialized x2184 with NULL instead of 0 and typed it as HSD_JObj*, matching its use with HSD_JObjUnref.
what_changed: Changed Fighter::x59C and Fighter::x5A0 from s32 to void* in src/melee/ft/fighter.h.
Changed Fighter::x5F0 from s32 to void* in src/melee/ft/fighter.h.
Changed Fighter::x2040 from s32 to void* in src/melee/ft/fighter.h.
Changed Fighter::x2184 from s32 to HSD_JObj* in src/melee/ft/fighter.h.
Updated Fighter_UnkInitLoad_80068914 to initialize fighter->x2184 with NULL instead of integer 0.
Removed explicit void* casts in Fighter_Unload_8006DABC calls to HSD_JObjUnref and HSD_ObjFree because the struct fields now have pointer-compatible types.
Preserved existing objAllocData bucket expressions and cleanup ordering in Fighter_Unload_8006DABC.
smart_moves: Used callsite behavior in Fighter_Unload_8006DABC as evidence for member types: fields passed to HSD_ObjFree became void*, while x2184 passed to HSD_JObjUnref became HSD_JObj*.
Improved type information in the shared Fighter struct rather than locally casting every use in the matched function.
Used NULL for pointer initialization at x2184, making pointer intent explicit and aligning the initializer with the new HSD_JObj* type.
Kept unknown allocation-backed members as void* instead of over-naming or inventing concrete struct types not proven by the excerpt.
decomp_lessons: When a newly matched function needs repeated casts for struct members, consider whether the struct field type is still an integer placeholder and should be refined globally.
Free/unref APIs are useful type oracles: HSD_JObjUnref indicates an HSD_JObj* field, while generic HSD_ObjFree arguments may justify void* until the pointed-to layout is known.
Prefer improving header types to suppressing compiler mismatches with casts; better struct types make future decompilation cleaner and more searchable.
For unknown heap allocations in decomp structs, void* is a conservative improvement over s32 when pointer usage is established but the allocation's real type is not.
naming_conventions: Unknown Fighter fields remained offset-based names such as x59C, x5A0, x5F0, x2040, and x2184.
Known or partially known fields retained descriptive suffixes, such as x5E8_fighterBones, x2D8_specialAttributes2, x20A0_accessory, x8AC_animSkeleton, and x2064_ledgeCooldown.
Pointer constants were expressed as NULL rather than 0 after the field was typed as a pointer.
HSD object pointer types follow project-style names such as HSD_JObj*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed explicit casts without changing function logic, a typical cleanup after struct type refinement for a matched function.
Maintained the same HSD_ObjFree objAllocData index expressions, such as objAllocData[(0xDC / 40)], while only changing argument types.
Used minimal header-only type refinement to preserve layout offsets while improving C source compatibility.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace Fighter void* fields x59C, x5A0, x5F0, or x2040 with concrete types.
Search for additional uses of Fighter::x2184 to confirm HSD_JObj ownership and cleanup behavior.
Look for other matched cleanup/unload functions where casts to void* indicate stale integer placeholder fields.
